decision_summary: The chip peak temperature (89.26°C) exceeds the 85.0°C limit. The most effective primary lever is to increase the heat spreader conductivity to enhance lateral heat distribution away from the chip hotspot. A conservative 40% increase is proposed, staying well within the allowed 2.0x step ratio and upper bound of 500.0 W/(m*K).

changes.reason: Increasing spreader conductivity improves lateral heat spreading, reducing the local temperature gradient under the chip and lowering the peak temperature.

expected_effects: Reduction in chip_max_temperature towards or below 85.0°C
More uniform temperature distribution across the heat spreader
risk_notes: Diminishing returns may occur if the bottleneck shifts to the base plate conductivity or boundary conditions.
High conductivity materials may be cost-prohibitive in real-world applications, though this is a simulation study.
